differences.title: 1. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Leverages Starlette for async, offering higher concurrency and better I/O bound performance.
differences.rightDesc: Relies on Werkzeug for WSGI, typically single-threaded unless explicitly managed.

differences.title: 2. TYPE HINTS
differences.leftDesc: Uses Python type hints for automatic data validation, serialization, and OpenAPI documentation.
differences.rightDesc: Relies on extensions like Marshmallow for validation and serialization.

differences.title: 3. DOCUMENTATION
differences.leftDesc: Generates interactive API docs (Swagger UI, ReDoc) directly from code and type hints.
differences.rightDesc: Requires manual setup of documentation tools or writing it separately.

differences.title: 4. DEPENDENCY INJECTION
differences.leftDesc: Features a powerful dependency injection system for easier testing and managing dependencies.
differences.rightDesc: Requires manual implementation or external libraries for dependency injection.

differences.title: 5. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: More features and concepts to grasp initially due to async and type system.
differences.rightDesc: Simpler core concepts, easier for beginners to get started quickly.

differences.title: 6. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Benefits from modern Python features and active community development.
differences.rightDesc: Extensive third-party extensions and a large, established community.

differences.title: 7. USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for building high-throughput, I/O-bound web services and microservices.
differences.rightDesc: Great for simple web apps, quick prototypes, and smaller services.
